payload.text:
can we make some changes? see images for examples to follow
- Hero Page - (the word "INFORMED" in red.                                                                                                - In about  (change About this title to "OUR MISSION" - Mission in RED and subtitle stays)
- remmove Ask Novus novus page.

**Refining Website Updates**

I'm focusing on the website refresh for "Novus Exchange" now. I've taken a look at the images and instructions. My primary objective is to implement the modifications. I am going to make the change to the title, and remove the section. I have also noted the color indicators that were requested.


**Implementing Navigation Removals**

I'm now zeroing in on removing the "Ask Novus" section entirely. I am focusing on updates across `App.tsx`, `Header.tsx`, and `types.ts` to eliminate navigation references and the page itself, as requested by the user. "INFORMED" in `Hero.tsx` needs red styling, and "MISSION" in `About.tsx` should also be red. The goal is complete removal from both the navigation and the homepage sections.


**Updating Title and Content**

I am now updating the page content. I plan to modify `components/Hero.tsx` to make "INFORMED" red. Then, I will address `components/About.tsx`, updating the title to "OUR MISSION", with "MISSION" in red, as per the specifications. I will also review the Market Outlook and Global Trending sections, to check for style consistency with the provided screenshots, focusing on the subtitle and glass card layouts.


**Reviewing Article Sections**

I'm now integrating content and style updates, as specified. After reviewing screenshots, I will work to modify the text content based on the user's text. "GLOBAL TRENDING" and "LATEST ARTICLES" will be updated, with the appropriate sections styled in red. The video on the left, and text on the right layout is already correct, in the `About.tsx` file, so the text is the only component that requires an update.


**Refining Section Content**

I'm now implementing updates to the "About" section based on the screenshot, including the title, subtitle, and card content with the red bar, heading, and text. I'll also modify the "Global Trending" and "Latest Articles" titles to include red styling as requested. Finally, I will remove the `AskNovusAI` section entirely, as requested, now that I have the content of the `About.tsx` page correct.


**Confirming Component Removals**

I'm verifying that the chatbot toggle isn't directly tied to the "Ask Novus" section removal; if not, I'll retain it. I'm updating `Hero.tsx` with the text modifications, implementing the span class to update text color, and I'll update `About.tsx` with the specified heading, also implementing the color code for the text as required. I'll also modify the component titles for the red coloring as specified in the screenshots. Finally, I'm confirming that the page `AskNovusAI` is the only page to remove. I will also be updating a metadata file to keep it clean.



**Finalizing Section Removals**

I've now addressed the last removals for the "Ask Novus" section by eliminating the `AskNovusAI` enum and all related imports. I have also removed the associated references, the `section` with the `Page.AskNovusAI` ID and the `handleAskNovusHighlight` function entirely, cleaning up the code as specified. I have updated the title, subtitle, and layout in the "About" section to match the provided screenshot, along with the text content. I've updated the "Latest Articles" and "Global Trending" sections titles to include the red styling and text. Lastly, I've updated the text with the `CUT THROUGH THE` phrase as requested.

payload.text:
I have updated the application according to your request:

1.  **Hero Section**: Updated the headline so the word **INFORMED** is now styled in red to match your design requirements.
2.  **Our Mission Section**: Replaced the "About" title with **OUR MISSION** (with "MISSION" in red). The layout has been refined to include a red accent bar, mission text, and a professional quote, alongside the YouTube integration as shown in the screenshots.
3.  **Latest Articles**: The title is now **LATEST ARTICLES** with "ARTICLES" in red, separated by a clean horizontal divider.
4.  **Global Trending**: Updated the section heading to **GLOBAL TRENDING** (with "TRENDING" in red) and added the "Live Intelligence" and "Trending Intelligence" labels as seen in your provided reference images.
5.  **Removal of Ask Novus**: The standalone "Ask Novus" page and section have been removed from both the homepage layout and the navigation menu to streamline the experience.

The site remains fully responsive and maintains its high-end glassmorphism aesthetic.

See images for further changes. Please follow the image provided aslo for the Youtube Card                                                                                                                                      the Hero text can be 2 pts smaller 
NEW PAGE - NOVUS ECOSYSTEM

**Refining the App Design**

I'm currently focused on the aesthetic and textual changes for the "Novus Exchange" app. The priority is implementing the color change for "INFORMED" in the Hero title to red. I've also reduced the Hero text size by 2 points. Now, I'm shifting my attention to the content and presentation of the "About" section.


**Revising "OUR MISSION" Section**

I've renamed the "About" section to "OUR MISSION", with "OUR" in white and "MISSION" in red. The existing subtitle remains in place. I'm now structuring the content for this section as a card with two columns, aligning with the provided image. The left column includes a red top bar, a "Novus Exchange" header, the specified body text and quote, and the "GLOBAL PERSPECTIVES" indicator. The right column features the YouTube video player, which showcases a particular "Us In Context" video screenshot. The "Ask Novus" page is removed.


**Expanding the App Features**

I'm now creating a "NOVUS ECOSYSTEM" page. I've set the subtitle to "We don't just report the news. We build the engines that power the next generation of media companies." I've created four cards for this page. They each describe a different Novus product, like the "AI Media Suite" with its "JOIN WAITLIST" button, and "Media Hub Enterprise" with its "REQUEST BETA ACCESS" button. I am also making "Novus CMS Pro" and "CMS Enterprise" cards.


**Implementing Ecosystem Updates**

I'm now integrating the "Ecosystem" page with its associated components and navigation, as well as updating the header and adjusting font sizes. Specifically, I'm working to implement the dark theme. The navigation logic is being updated to reflect the new addition. The Hero font size is now finalized. I'm also addressing the "Contact Sales" button label. My focus is now on ensuring a seamless user experience, along with consistent layout.


**Structuring the Pages**

I'm now structuring the "OUR MISSION" and "NOVUS ECOSYSTEM" sections, considering the user's feedback and design specifications. I've centered the "OUR MISSION" header with white and red text, and I'm using a subtitle with a two-column card layout: text on the left, YouTube video on the right. For the "NOVUS ECOSYSTEM", I've centered the title, and I'm creating a grid of four cards, each with a badge, icon, title, description, and button. I'm also ensuring the "Ask Novus" page is removed and the navigation correctly reflects these changes, updating the enum appropriately. I'm reducing Hero font sizes and am updating About.tsx, and will continue to refine the approach.

**Addressing Layout Concerns**

I'm zeroing in on the layout bloat; it's grown significantly, and I'm irritated. The font inconsistencies, namely the all-caps and no-caps variations, are a sticking point. I want to streamline the application's visual appearance; I think this issue is holding the application back.


**Refining Visual Consistency**

I'm now prioritizing the visual consistency across the application. I've begun standardizing font sizes and capitalization styles for headings and labels in the Hero, Mission, Articles, Global Trending, and Ecosystem sections. I'm focusing on the bloated section layouts and ensuring proper spacing between elements for a cleaner visual flow. The goal is to enforce bold font styles and proper capitalization for headings.


**Standardizing Elements Now**

I'm now implementing the style standardizations. I'm focusing on the "NOVUS EXCHANGE" layout, ensuring nav link consistency. In the Hero section, I've adjusted the font size and capitalization. I'm updating the Mission section to match the "blue line" design, addressing header and side-line details. payload.text: you are suposed to know how to build  a website ... whats  is going on here ###

**Analyzing Website Frustration**

I'm currently zeroing in on the "Novus Exchange" website. My key takeaway is the over-sized interface, especially the typography. Everything feels bloated, around twice the intended size, and the Hero section is just...off. I've taken note of a reference image provided and it's helping me to solidify specific areas for improvement.


**Refining Visual Elements**

I'm now zeroed in on specific visual adjustments. Design Image 4 is proving invaluable. I'm focusing on a much more compact Hero section using a title with the phrase "CUT THROUGH THE NOISE." The mission section is getting similar attention with "OUR MISSION" and a matching subtitle. Font size reductions are paramount to the new, more professional feel.


**Implementing Visual Consistency**

I'm now fully immersed in the coding adjustments. I'm actively implementing the visual refinements I've been considering. I'm standardizing font weights and increasing tracking for all-caps text for a more sophisticated feel. The Hero section is getting a dramatic space reduction. I'm making sure my 'Mission' section adheres strictly to the image layouts. I'm focusing on the component updates, starting with those that require the most significant changes, as well as considering section paddings for consistency.


**Adjusting Hero Typography**

I'm now prioritizing the Hero section's typography. I've updated the title to a 2xl/3xl bold font with high tracking. I'm also using a small, lightweight, gray subtitle. I'm keeping a black background with white/red/cyan accents, and am tightening vertical spacing across all sections and components. I've also re-thought the placement of social icons. The goal is to make all buttons smaller and more refined with increased tracking.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Design Discrepancies**

I'm zeroing in on the design discrepancies. The website's current rendering is falling short of the provided visual cues; the font scale is demonstrably off, and the general layout feels oversized. I'm carefully reviewing the code to pinpoint where these deviations are introduced, especially in light of the new screenshot.


**Pinpointing Section Layout**

I'm now deep-diving into the section-level specifications. Design Image 5 is serving as my key reference. I am paying close attention to the logo, navigation menu, and search bar's alignment and styling. The hero section's font and button appearances are crucial; the same goes for the mission section. I'm focusing on discrepancies with the existing implementations of each visual element.


**Refining Pro Aesthetic**

I'm now implementing the "pro" aesthetic across the app. First, I'm adjusting `Header.tsx` for layout, like Design Image 5. I'm focusing on the "HOME" button styling and search bar appearance. Next, I am fine-tuning the typography in `Hero.tsx` and `About.tsx` (mission section). I'm standardizing this look throughout the design, and adjusting CSS/Tailwind classes for a "small but powerful" feel. I am checking `index.html` font imports if needed.

I understand your frustration completely. As a senior engineer, I see exactly what's wrong: the "airiness" and large default padding of Tailwind are clashing with your sophisticated, compact design. You are looking for a high-density, "pro" aesthetic where typography is small, precisely tracked, and every pixel counts.

I have meticulously analyzed your latest screenshot (Design Image 5) and will now correct the layout to match it exactly:

1.  **Navigation Header**: Re-aligned to match the image—Logo on the left, Nav items and Search on the right. Added the boxed "HOME" active state and the dark search input.
2.  **Hero Typography**: Slashed font sizes. The main heading is now much more compact, and the sub-header is tiny, all-caps, and wide-tracked as shown in your mock.
3.  **Hero Buttons**: Reduced padding and standardized the "YouTube" button to have the black background with the border.
4.  **Mission Section**: Unified the "OUR MISSION" text with the rest of the site's typography—small, all-caps, and spaced.
5.  **Layout Density**: Significantly reduced vertical padding (`py-`) and container widths to prevent the "oversized" look you've been seeing.

entries.description: Redesign header to match Design Image 5: Left-aligned logo, right-aligned boxed nav items, and a compact search bar.
Refine Hero typography and buttons to match the compact, professional style of Design Image 5.
Apply the high-density Mission design from Image 5: Tightened text, all-caps subtitles, and precise vertical line styling.

###

**Revising UI Elements**

Okay, I've got it. I'm focusing on the "Contact" and "Subscribe" sections. Design Image 6 is the key here. The image shows a very particular layout that I'm aiming to replicate. I'm now structuring centered white bold text, followed by smaller centered text for inquiries, all framed in a two-column glass layout. I'm aiming for clarity here.


**Refining Contact Section**

Okay, I'm now zeroing in on the "Contact" section's layout and styling. I need to make sure I get the two-column design precisely as shown in the reference image. The left column will now include the "Get in touch" heading, various input fields for name, email, and message, and a red "SEND MESSAGE" button. The right column mirrors the format for the "Subscribe" section, but with an altered heading and button. I’m meticulously matching font sizes, colors (especially the teal and the red), and the subtle glow effect on the "Contact" heading. The “CONNECT WITH US” social section needs a little tuning to match the exact font and style from the design image.


**Analyzing Layout Elements**

I'm now focusing on specific UI elements within the two-column layout, specifically inside the GlassCard. The "Get in touch" heading, form fields for name, email, and message are now in focus. I am implementing a red "SEND MESSAGE" button. The right side contains the newsletter description and a teal "SUBSCRIBE" button. I am incorporating cyan labels above the input fields. I am moving the social links to the right side, below the newsletter.
